source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage/Crane Incident

event.top_event: Tubular pipe dropped during hoisting operations striking worker
actions_taken: Medical evaluation at Pelican State Outpatient
Incident reported to BSEE Lafayette District
Root Cause Analysis conducted by WES
G-110 Incident of Noncompliance issued to Fieldwood
event.summary: On 17 February 2020 at 07:55 hours, a Warrior Energy Services Electric Line Helper sustained a foot injury when a 1.25-inch tubular pipe slipped through single joint elevators during hoisting operations on a Fieldwood Energy platform at South Marsh Island Block 149D. The tubular fell and struck the worker's right foot, causing bruising. The root cause was determined to be improper equipment: the elevators were pre-dressed with dies sized for 1.5-inch pipe instead of 1.25-inch pipe. The elevator C-plate was installed upside down, obscuring the size stamp that would have allowed crew verification.
recommendations: BSEE Lafayette District recommended issuance of a Safety Alert for this incident
key_phrases: elevators not inspected prior to deployment
failed to oversee safe and workmanlike manner

threats.name: Tubular pipe slips through undersized elevator dies
Crew unable to verify elevator compatibility due to obscured size stamp
Defective equipment deployed without field inspection

consequences.name: Worker struck by falling tubular pipe
Foot injury with bruising and swelling
Worker evacuation and medical treatment required

controls.name: Equipment pre-job inspection and verification
controls.barrier_role: Verify elevator dies match tubular size requirements
controls.lod_basis: Initial control to prevent use of incompatible equipment

supporting_text: The on-site P&A crew did not effectively verify the elevators were compatible with the tubular prior to use
the elevators were not inspected prior to deployment in the field nor were the elevators inspected in the field

controls.name: Visual identification of elevator die size via C-plate stamp
controls.barrier_role: Allow crew to quickly verify correct elevator size
controls.lod_basis: Design feature to enable rapid visual verification

supporting_text: Typically, crew members can quickly check the intended size by observing the size stamp on the C-plate
with the elevator C-plate installed upside down, the elevator die size stamp was not viewable as assembled

controls.name: Pre-job planning for correct equipment specification
controls.barrier_role: Ensure correct elevator dies are specified and procured for the job
controls.lod_basis: Planning control to prevent procurement of wrong equipment

supporting_text: inadequate pre-job planning for the determination of the correct elevator dies

controls.name: Operator oversight of safe and workmanlike operations
controls.barrier_role: Ensure platform operations are conducted safely and in compliance with standards
controls.lod_basis: Management control to oversee contractor performance

supporting_text: Fieldwood Energy LLC., (Fieldwood) failed to oversee that operations were performed in a safe and workmanlike manner on Fieldwood's platform

controls.name: Proper tailing and control of lifted tubular
controls.barrier_role: Prevent tubular from slipping and falling during hoisting
controls.lod_basis: Last-line control to prevent dropped object consequence

supporting_text: a WES E/L Helper tailed the tubular with a piece of rope
the tubular slipped through the closed elevators and fell back bouncing off the pipe rack

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
